<command-message>erk:one-shot</command-message> <command-name>/erk:one-shot</command-name> <command-args>
with our new command structure all copy like this: ---- Checking PR status... PR #8128: Simplify objective roadmap to single-reference (PR-only) model with deterministic re-rendering (OPEN) Base branch: master
Triggering pr-fix-conflicts workflow... Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) ✓ Workflow triggered ⚠ Plan #8128 has incomplete plan-header (missing created_at, created_by, schema_version), skipping dispatch metadata update
Run URL: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22391870130
----
should say Dispatched inte of Triggered. audit all copy</command-args>

# One-Shot Autonomous Execution
Submit a task for fully autonomous remote execution. The prompt will be dispatched to a GitHub Actions workflow where Claude autonomously explores, plans, implements, and creates a PR.
## Steps
1. Validate that `
with our new command structure all copy like this: ---- Checking PR status... PR #8128: Simplify objective roadmap to single-reference (PR-only) model with deterministic re-rendering (OPEN) Base branch: master
Triggering pr-fix-conflicts workflow... Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) ✓ Workflow triggered ⚠ Plan #8128 has incomplete plan-header (missing created_at, created_by, schema_version), skipping dispatch metadata update
Run URL: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22391870130
----
should say Dispatched inte of Triggered. audit all copy` is non-empty. If empty, tell the user they need to provide a prompt.
2. Write the prompt text to a scratch file to avoid shell quoting issues with long or complex prompts:
```bash # Write prompt to session-scoped scratch file mkdir -p .erk/scratch/sessions/f8ea88c2-0331-4c46-8bcd-bf1e99daaebc cat > .erk/scratch/sessions/f8ea88c2-0331-4c46-8bcd-bf1e99daaebc/one-shot-prompt.md << 'PROMPT_EOF'
with our new command structure all copy like this: ---- Checking PR status... PR #8128: Simplify objective roadmap to single-reference (PR-only) model with deterministic re-rendering (OPEN) Base branch: master
Triggering pr-fix-conflicts workflow... Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) ✓ Workflow triggered ⚠ Plan #8128 has incomplete plan-header (missing created_at, created_by, schema_version), skipping dispatch metadata update
Run URL: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22391870130
----
should say Dispatched inte of Triggered. audit all copy PROMPT_EOF ```
3. Run the CLI command with --file:
```bash erk one-shot --file .erk/scratch/sessions/f8ea88c2-0331-4c46-8bcd-bf1e99daaebc/one-shot-prompt.md ```
4. Clean up the scratch file:
```bash rm -f .erk/scratch/sessions/f8ea88c2-0331-4c46-8bcd-bf1e99daaebc/one-shot-prompt.md ```
5. Display the output to the user, which includes the PR URL and workflow run URL.
If the command fails, display the error message.

session.assistant: The `erk plan check` command was removed in commit `a4114a802` ("Unify PR and plan CLI commands under `erk pr` group") but the workflow wasn't updated. Let me see what the command was renamed to.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git show a4114a802 --stat | grep -i check | head -10
Check which check files were affected in the unify PR

session.tool_result: Consolidates `erk plan submit`, `erk plan check`, and `erk plan co` under the `erk pr` command group with universal lifecycle stages (check, checkout, dispatch). This creates a cohesive CLI where the `pr` group handles checking, checking out, and dispatching both pull requests and plans. - `src/erk/cli/commands/plan/check_cmd.py` - Merged into `pr/check_cmd.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/plan/checkout_cmd.py` - Merged into `pr/checkout_cmd.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/` - Merged plan functionality into check_cmd.py, checkout_cmd.py; added dispatch_cmd.py - **Command consolidation**: Moves `erk plan submit → erk pr dispatch`, `erk plan check → erk pr check`, `erk plan co → erk pr checkout` into unified `erk pr` group - **Universal checkout routing**: `erk pr checkout` now handles both PR references (plain numbers, PR URLs) and plan references (P-prefix, issue URLs) with smart parsing - **Extended plan validation**: `erk pr check` now accepts optional identifier to validate plan format (merged from `plan check` command) - **Simplified plan group**: `erk plan` retains only `dup-check`, `docs`, and `learn` subcommands erk plan check 456 # Validate plan format erk plan co P789 # Checkout plan branch

Usage: erk pr [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
Manage pull requests.
Options: -h, --help Show this message and exit.
Commands: address Address PR review comments with AI-powered resolution. check Validate PR rules or plan format. checkout Checkout PR or plan into a worktree for review. close Close a plan by issue number or GitHub URL. co Checkout PR or plan into a worktree for review. create Create a plan issue from markdown content. dispatch Dispatch plans for remote AI implementation via GitHub... fix-conflicts Fix merge conflicts with AI-powered resolution. list List plans as a static table. log Display chronological event log for a plan. replan Replan existing erk-plan issue(s) against current... rewrite Squash, regenerate AI commit message, push, and update... submit Submit PR with AI-generated commit message. sync Synchronize current PR branch with remote base. sync-divergence Sync branch with remote and resolve divergence. view Fetch and display a plan by identifier.
session.assistant: Got it. The command was renamed from `erk plan check` to `erk pr check` in the unification PR, but the `one-shot.yml` workflow was never updated. The fix is straightforward:
session.tool_result: 185→ - name: Validate plan format 186→ if: steps.plan.outputs.plan_success == 'true' && steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id != '' 187→ env: 188→ GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }} 189→ run: | 190→ erk plan check ${{ steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id }} 191→

PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/optimized-painting-frost.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Fix: Stale `erk plan check` reference in one-shot workflow Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master) (plan:#7977)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create a plan PR" (Recommended) - Create a draft PR with the plan and stop. Does NOT proceed to implementation. 2. "Skip PR and implement here" - Skip creating a PR, implement directly in current worktree (for small PR iterations that don't need PR tracking). 3. "View/Edit the plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage editing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create a plan PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. STOP - Do NOT call ExitPlanMode. The plan-save command handles everything. Stay in plan mode and let the user exit manually if desired.
If user chooses 'Skip PR and implement here': 1. Create implement-now marker (skip PR creation): erk exec marker create --session-id f8ea88c2-0331-4c46-8bcd-bf1e99daaebc \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly

session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
---
## Fix: Stale `erk plan check` reference in one-shot workflow
### Context
The one-shot workflow (`one-shot.yml`) fails at the "Validate plan format" step because it calls `erk plan check`, which was renamed to `erk pr check` in PR #8110 ("Unify PR and plan CLI commands under `erk pr` group"). The workflow was not updated during that rename.
This is the sole cause of the one-shot failure (run 22391925318).
### Change
**File:** `.github/workflows/one-shot.yml` (line 190)
```diff - erk plan check ${{ steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id }} + erk pr check ${{ steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id }} ```
No other stale `erk plan check/submit/co` references exist in `.github/`.
### Verification
Re-run the failed one-shot workflow or dispatch a new one-shot to confirm the "Validate plan format" step passes.
---

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Fix: Stale `erk plan check` reference in one-shot workflow\nCurrent conte...[truncated 455 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Fix: Stale `erk plan check` reference in one-shot workflow Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master) (plan:#7977)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create a plan PR (Recommended)". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
Launching skill: erk:plan-save
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) ```
Objective linking is automatic: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command.
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. - **Issue backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "github"`): Creates a GitHub issue. Metadata in the issue body, plan content in the first comment.
The JSON output contract is the same for both backends (`plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`).
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type` flag:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 1.5: Generate Branch Slug
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 2: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, branch slug, and optional flags:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="f8ea88c2-0331-4c46-8bcd-bf1e99daaebc" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 3.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 3: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 2.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close <"draft PR" if plan_backend=="draft_pr", else "issue"> #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 3.5: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "f8ea88c2-0331-4c46-8bcd-bf1e99daaebc" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --plan "#<plan_number>" ```
This atomically fetches the issue body, finds the matching node row, updates the Plan cell, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 4: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
If `branch_name` is present in the JSON, display the same next-steps block as the success case below (using the `plan_backend` field to choose the correct format).
If `branch_name` is absent, display only: `View PR: <plan_url>`
Return immediately (skip Steps 3, 3.5 above if not already executed).
**Otherwise, on success**, display based on `plan_backend` from JSON output:
**Header (both backends):**
``` Plan "<title>" saved as <"draft PR" if plan_backend=="draft_pr", else "issue"> #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
**Slot options block (used by both backends below):**
The "OR exit Claude Code first" section should show both slot allocation options, with the recommended one listed first based on trunk detection:
If **on trunk = true**:
``` OR exit Claude Code first, then run one of:
New slot (recommended — you're on trunk): Local: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan <plan_number> Implement: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan <plan_number> --script)" && erk implement --dangerous
Same slot: Local: erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> Implement: source "$(erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> --script)" && erk implement --dangerous
Dispatch to Queue: erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If **on trunk = false**:
``` OR exit Claude Code first, then run one of:
Same slot (recommended — you're in a slot): Local: erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> Implement: source "$(erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> --script)" && erk implement --dangerous
New slot: Local: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan <plan_number> Implement: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan <plan_number> --script)" && erk implement --dangerous
Dispatch to Queue: erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
**If `plan_backend` is `"draft_pr"`:**
``` Next steps:
View PR: <plan_url>
In Claude Code: Dispatch to queue: /erk:pr-dispatch — Dispatch plan for remote agent implementation
OR exit Claude Code first, then run one of: Checkout: erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> Dispatch to Queue: erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
**If `plan_backend` is `"github"` (or absent):**
``` Next steps:
View Issue: <plan_url>
In Claude Code: Dispatch to queue: /erk:pr-dispatch — Dispatch plan for remote agent implementation Plan review: /erk:plan-review — Submit plan as PR for human review before implementation
OR exit Claude Code first, then run one of: Checkout: erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> Dispatch to Queue: erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
If the JSON output contains `slot_name` and `slot_objective_updated: true`, also display: `Slot objective updated: <slot_name> → #<objective-number>`
**Note:** Slot objective updates are handled automatically by `plan-save` when an objective is linked via the session marker - no separate command call needed.
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the issue number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved issue number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "f8ea88c2-0331-4c46-8bcd-bf1e99daaebc" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the issue number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.thinking:
Let me follow the plan-save instructions. First, I need to read the plan file to get the title, generate a branch slug, then run the save command.
The plan title from the plan file is: "Fix: Stale `erk plan check` reference in one-shot workflow"
Branch slug: "fix-oneshot-plan-check" - that captures the essence well.

View PR: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8148

OR exit Claude Code first, then run one of:
New slot (recommended — you're on trunk): Local: `erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8148` Implement: `source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8148 --script)" && erk implement --dangerous`
Same slot: Local: `erk br co --for-plan 8148` Implement: `source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8148 --script)" && erk implement --dangerous`
Dispatch to Queue: `erk pr dispatch 8148`
session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
